published_prior_art.cite: Hahn1998Econometrica, HiranoImbensRidder2003Econometrica, ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML: standard unconfounded ATE EIF/AIPW/Riesz baseline

repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1 targets weak-overlap studentization, not an LRO weighted-GMM normalization frontier

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO + Hansen1982Econometrica + Hahn1998Econometrica: general LRO/GMM aggregation plus known ATE EIF make the current iff mostly an ATE specialization
none; diagnostic algebra is not presented as a published-estimator refutation

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: as written the kernel is a direct ATE specialization of published LRO/GMM aggregation and known ATE EIF theory; to reach flagship it needs a non-tautological primitive frontier for a specific published LRO normalization, not just alpha_J equals alpha_0.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The iff uses alpha_J=alpha_0 in L2(P_DX), but report equivalence only identifies alpha differences on cells with positive residual variance; use the sigma^2-weighted L2 quotient or assume positive Var(Y|D,X) on all support cells.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The class J=(b_J,A_J,W_J) is defined algebraically but not restricted to actual LRO FSIF/moment normalizations, so the named-published-estimator claim is underpinned by an arbitrary report class.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the equivalence condition by equality in the residual-variance-weighted L2 space, or add a full-support positive residual-variance assumption and adjust K_J/C_J accordingly.

Add primitive LRO admissibility restrictions tying b_J and A_J to the Chernozhukov-Escanciano-Ichimura-Newey-Robins FSIF equations, so J_ow is a genuine published LRO specialization rather than an arbitrary loading.

To plausibly reach flagship, derive a concrete observable threshold for the optimal-weight ATE LRO normalization under a named moment dictionary and show both equivalence and strict non-equivalence regimes.

recommended_next_step: Patch Conjecture 1's quotient-space condition and LRO-admissibility class, then sharpen the optimal-weight frontier into a primitive non-tautological threshold.
